title: Enrich triage task exports with context and backlinks

content.title: Enrich triage task exports with context and backlinks
content.summary: After exporting T-0056/T-0057/T-0058 as GitHub Issues, the issues were evaluated as bare-bones stubs. A grill session resolved how triage task exports should be enriched. Triage issues are intentional stubs, not specs — they are enriched when re-ingested via a grill session. The to-tasks skill is responsible for embedding per-item context and dedicated backlink fields (seed_ref, task_ref) on triage task items. export-tasks renders the backlink footer only when wall != 'corporate'. All decisions resolved; seed is ready.
content.problem_statement: Triage tasks exported to GitHub Issues or Notion carried only the deferred item text, losing the surrounding context from the grill session. When these items are re-ingested in a future session, that context is gone unless it was preserved at export time.
content.solution: to-tasks populates per-item context (from deferred[].context in the seed) and dedicated backlink fields (seed_ref, task_ref as filename-only strings) on each triage task item. export-tasks renders a backlink footer block in the issue/page body when wall != 'corporate'. The description stays clean; the backlink is a separate dedicated field.
decisions: Exported triage issues are intentional stubs, not specs. Enrichment happens when they are re-ingested via a grill session.
to-tasks is responsible for enriching triage task descriptions with per-item context and backlink fields.
Only the per-item deferred[].context field is included in the triage task description — not the session summary. The session summary would duplicate across all items from the same session.
Backlinks use filename-only references (no directory prefix). This makes them path-agnostic and archival-safe regardless of future directory reorganization.
Triage task items carry two new dedicated fields: seed_ref (seed filename, string) and task_ref (task file filename, string). These are separate from the description field.
export-tasks renders a backlink footer block (seed: <filename>, task: <filename>, task_id: <id>) in the issue/page body only when wall != 'corporate'.
Corporate-wall items receive no backlinks. Backlinks are suppressed for corporate-domain work to avoid exposing personal context in corporate systems.
source.ref in task files switches to filename-only convention, matching the backlink standard and preparing for the same archival moves.
Condensed or merged seed files carry a condensed_from: [...] field listing the original filenames as aliases, preserving archival fidelity.
grill-me session-end protocol: print a plain text recommendation instead of using AskUserQuestion, which hard-blocks the terminal UI.
implementation_decisions: task-schema.json gains two optional string fields on task items: seed_ref and task_ref.

to-tasks: when emitting a triage task from a deferred[] item, set description = deferred.text + (deferred.context if present), set seed_ref = seed filename (basename only), set task_ref = output task file filename (basename only).

export-tasks: after resolving the adapter for a triage item, check wall value from task-routing.json. If wall != 'corporate', append the backlink footer block to the issue/page body: '---
seed: <seed_ref>
task: <task_ref>
task_id: <id>'.
Condensed seed files: add condensed_from: [<filename>, ...] as an optional field in the seed schema to list original filenames when multiple seeds are merged.
source.ref in task files: to-tasks writes only the basename (e.g. '20260606-0118-dispatch-tasks-pipeline.json'), not a relative path.

deferred.text: Corporate triage item enrichment and backlink strategy
deferred.rationale: The user deferred this conversation explicitly. Corporate items have different context-sharing constraints.
deferred.context: Personal-wall items now carry seed_ref + task_ref backlinks and per-item context. Corporate-wall items are stripped of backlinks at export time. The open question is whether corporate items should receive any enrichment at all, or whether a separate enrichment path exists (e.g. Linear description fields populated differently).

deferred.rationale: The user wants to modify clean-scaffolding to archive rather than delete, but the archival directory structure is not yet decided.
deferred.context: Currently clean-scaffolding deletes seeds, tasks, handoffs. With backlinks now pointing to filenames, archival moves are safe (filename-only refs resolve via find). The user is considering a Django-migration-style condensation approach but has not settled the structure. condensed_from field in seed schema is a prerequisite for this.

out_of_scope.text: Fully speccing triage issues at export time
out_of_scope.rationale: Triage issues are intentional stubs. Speccing them at export time would be premature — they haven't been grilled for the underlying work yet.

out_of_scope.text: Including session summary in triage task descriptions
out_of_scope.rationale: Session summary is shared across all deferred items from a session. Including it would duplicate the same boilerplate in every issue from that session.

out_of_scope.text: Embedding the backlink block inside the description field
out_of_scope.rationale: Embedding in description requires fragile text surgery to strip for corporate items. Dedicated fields (seed_ref, task_ref) allow clean conditional omission by export-tasks.

out_of_scope.text: Including the full decisions[] array from the seed in triage issue body
out_of_scope.rationale: Decisions array could be 15-20 items. Including all would be noisy and mostly irrelevant to any specific deferred item. The backlink provides lazy-load access to the full context on demand.

risks_and_tradeoffs: Filename-only refs require find-by-name resolution at consumption time. If two seeds share the same filename (should be impossible with YYYYMMDD-HHMM-slug naming), resolution is ambiguous.
condensed_from aliases require the consuming tool to check aliases when a direct filename match fails. This is a lookup convention, not enforced by schema.

wall != 'corporate' condition for backlinks includes project-wall items (e.g. spawned-sapien). Whether project-wall items should also be backlink-suppressed was not discussed in this session and may need revisiting.
content.next_action: Run /to-tasks to generate implementation tasks for the to-tasks and export-tasks enrichment changes.

tasks.title: Add seed_ref, task_ref, and condensed_from to JSON schemas
tasks.description: Update task-schema.json to add two optional string fields (seed_ref, task_ref) to task item properties. Update seed-schema.json to add an optional condensed_from field (array of strings). Both changes are backward-compatible — existing files without these fields still pass validation.
acceptance_criteria: task-schema.json defines seed_ref and task_ref as optional string properties in the task items block
seed-schema.json defines condensed_from as an optional array of strings
validate-schema.sh passes with exit 0 on all existing files in docs/tasks/
validate-schema.sh passes with exit 0 on all existing files in docs/seeds/

tasks.title: Update to-tasks to enrich triage emission with context and backlinks
tasks.description: Update to-tasks SKILL.md step 3a to enrich triage task items emitted from deferred[] entries: set description = deferred.text + newline + deferred.context (if present); set seed_ref = seed filename basename only (no directory prefix); set task_ref = output task file filename basename only. Also update step 6: source.ref is written as basename only (e.g. '20260606-1550-foo.json'), not a relative path.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md step 3a instructs: description = deferred.text + deferred.context (if present, separated by newline), seed_ref = seed basename, task_ref = output task file basename
SKILL.md step 6 instructs: source.ref is the seed filename basename only
A triage task emitted from a deferred[] item that has a context field carries that context appended to its description

tasks.title: Render backlink footer in export-tasks for non-corporate wall items
tasks.description:
Update export-tasks (agent and/or adapter scripts) to append a backlink footer to the issue/page body for triage items where the destination wall is not 'corporate'. After resolving the adapter for a triage item, read the wall value from task-routing.json using the item's domain. If wall != 'corporate', append the following after the description body: '---
seed: <seed_ref>
task: <task_ref>
task_id: <id>'. Corporate-wall items (standard-metrics) receive no backlinks — suppressed entirely.
acceptance_criteria: export-tasks appends the backlink footer for personal-wall and project-wall items
export-tasks omits the backlink footer for corporate-wall items
wall value is read from task-routing.json destinations[domain].wall, not hardcoded

tasks.title: Corporate triage item enrichment and backlink strategy
tasks.description:
Corporate triage item enrichment and backlink strategy.

Personal-wall items now carry seed_ref + task_ref backlinks and per-item context. Corporate-wall items are stripped of backlinks at export time. The open question is whether corporate items should receive any enrichment at all, or whether a separate enrichment path exists (e.g. Linear description fields populated differently).
acceptance_criteria: Item has been exported to its destination by export-tasks

tasks.title: clean-scaffolding archival structure
tasks.description:
clean-scaffolding archival structure.

Currently clean-scaffolding deletes seeds, tasks, handoffs. With backlinks now pointing to filenames, archival moves are safe (filename-only refs resolve via find). The user is considering a Django-migration-style condensation approach but has not settled the structure. condensed_from field in seed schema is a prerequisite for this.
acceptance_criteria: Item has been exported to its destination by export-tasks
